head.hi: Memorandum Dictated in the Office of the Assistant Secretary of State by the British Ambassador
head.note: Paraphrase of a telegram from The Hague, dated July 16, 1917.

div.p: Austrian Legation stated that in the course of the negotiations between the Emperor of Austria and Kaiser Wilhelm, Hindenburg had declared that under no circumstances could Germany adopt a program of a peace without annexation. Disruption and national bankruptcy would be the necessary consequence of a peace in which Germany recognized the commercial independence of Belgium.
